slides.title: Late dinners are stealing your *deep sleep*.
slides.sub: It's not the calories — it's the clock. The hour you eat is talking to your body in a language you've been ignoring.

slides.title: Your body keeps time
slides.body: Late meals fight rising melatonin, which can mean *worse sleep and glucose* — your gut handles food best earlier in the day.

slides.title: Eat big earlier
slides.body: Shift more food to *breakfast and lunch*, less at night. Many people report steadier energy that actually matches their day.

slides.title: Stop eating before bed
slides.body: Finishing *3 hours before bed* may help you fall into deeper sleep with less late-night reflux.

slides.title: Use morning light
slides.body: *Morning light plus breakfast* set your internal clock, telling your body the day has started so night comes easier.

slides.title: Keep meals on schedule
slides.body: Random eating times confuse the rhythm. *Eating on a schedule* keeps energy and sleep predictable instead of all over the place.

slides.title: Time your meals, *win the night*.
slides.body: Log your meals and sleep in Vibe Health and the trend line shows how dinner timing moves your morning energy.

caption:
Late dinners are stealing your deep sleep — and it's the clock, not the calories. 🕗

Front-load your food earlier, stop eating about 3 hours before bed, anchor with morning light, keep meals on a schedule. Save it. 🔖

Log meals and sleep in Vibe Health and watch the trend line connect them → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Late Dinners Steal Your Deep Sleep
